preamble: The transformation from the voiced alveolar fricative /z/ to the voiced dental fricative /ð/ involves a shift in the place of articulation, moving the point of contact from the alveolar ridge to the teeth. This process, known as dentalization, results in a sound produced with the tongue tip or blade against the upper front teeth.

examples.note: 'I pour' (from Standard Persian to Zoroastrian Persian)

sources: Bernard, C. (2020). A newly discovered Persian variety: the case of “Zoroastrian Persian”. Orientalia Suecana, 69, 57–67.
